event: King of Scots and Andrea Browne
symbols: crown on king's head — royal authority and temporal power
monk's brown habit — religious devotion and monastic humility
arched windows with twilight view — the liminal space between day and night, decision and consequence
tapestry behind — the fabric of court life and hidden machinations
hanging oil lamp — light in darkness, the illumination of truth or conscience
description: An illustration showing a crowned figure, the King of Scots, standing prominently in a medieval castle interior. He wears a brown robe and a golden crown, with a commanding posture. To his left, partially hidden behind a curtain or tapestry, is a smaller figure, possibly a child or attendant. At his feet to the right, a figure in a monk's brown habit sits or kneels. The background shows arched windows through which a twilight landscape with trees is visible. A hanging oil lamp illuminates the scene from above. Rich golden tapestry dominates the left side.

_meta.title: King of Scots and Andrea Browne
